item.title: Flying Fijians put recent defeats behind them ahead of Scotland clash
item.description: More than 51,000 fans are expected to fill Murrayfield Stadium tomorrow as the FIJI Water Flying Fijians prepare to face Scotland, and Fiji players are ready. Flying Fijian Captain Tevita Ikanivere says despite losses to Wales and a heavy defeat to England, the Flying Fijians have put those results behind them and are determined to deliver a strong performance against Scotland.
item.content:encoded:
More than 51,000 fans are expected to fill Murrayfield Stadium tomorrow as the FIJI Water Flying Fijians prepare to face Scotland, and Fiji players are ready.

Flying Fijian Captain Tevita Ikanivere says despite losses to Wales and a heavy defeat to England, the Flying Fijians have put those results behind them and are determined to deliver a strong performance against Scotland.

Ikanivere says the players are fully switched on and ready for the challenge.

The Japan-based hooker says valuable lessons have been learned from their mistakes in the last two matches.

Ikanivere also says the weather conditions are ideal for rugby and the players are mentally prepared to perform in front of a crowd of more than 51,000 supporters.

Fiji will play Scotland at 1.10am tomorrow.

In other matches, the All Blacks will host Ireland at 7.10pm tomorrow, Japan will take on France at 8.40pm, and the Wallabies will face Italy at 10.10pm.

On Sunday, South Africa will meet Wales at 3.40am, while England will take on Argentina at 7.10am.

item.title: Vodafone Southern Zone Rugby: Marist and Nasinu set up U15 final while MGM and Nasinu book U16 showdown
item.description: Mikaele Liga , Pita Toganivalu · 18/07/2026 Marist Brothers High School began the Vodafone Southern Zone semi-finals on a high, beating Gospel High School 16-7 in the under-15 grade at the Suva Grammar School grounds.
item.content:encoded:
Mikaele Liga , Pita Toganivalu · 18/07/2026

Marist Brothers High School began the Vodafone Southern Zone semi-finals on a high, beating Gospel High School 16-7 in the under-15 grade at the Suva Grammar School grounds.

Nasinu Secondary School edged Grammar in the other under-15 semi-final 20-17 to book a place in the final against Marist next week.

Suva Grammar School's U16 rugby team lost the semi-final against a spirited Mahatma Gandhi Memorial High School 18-11 at the Suva Grammar ground today.

In the other U16 match, Nasinu edged Marist 16-15 to book a place in the final against MGM.

item.title: Kiran rallies behind Fiji Women's Drua ahead of semi final showdown
item.description: The countdown is almost over as the McDonald's Fiji Women's Drua prepare to face the Queensland Reds in a blockbuster Super W semi final at Churchill Park in Lautoka today. With a place in the grand final on the line, the defending champions will be looking to draw on home support, determination, and the resilience that has defined their campaign.
item.content:encoded:
The countdown is almost over as the McDonald's Fiji Women's Drua prepare to face the Queensland Reds in a blockbuster Super W semi final at Churchill Park in Lautoka today.

With a place in the grand final on the line, the defending champions will be looking to draw on home support, determination, and the resilience that has defined their campaign.

Fans are expected to turn out in numbers to cheer on the Drua Women as they aim to continue their impressive run in front of a passionate Fijian crowd.

Adding to the excitement, Minister for Women, Children and Social Protection, Sashi Kiran, has extended her heartfelt best wishes to the team ahead of the crucial clash.

Kiran commended the players for inspiring young girls and women across Fiji through their dedication, discipline, and outstanding performances on the rugby field.

The semi final is expected to be a fiercely contested encounter, with both teams eager to secure a place in the Super W final.

The Drua Women taken on Reds at Churchill Park at 1pm.

In the second semi final, the Western Force face Waratahs at 6.05pm today.

item.title: Seruvakula confident Flying Fijians will bounce back
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 17/07/2026 FIJI Water Flying Fijians Acting Head Coach Senirusi Seruvakula says he is confident the players will deliver a strong performance when they take on Scotland in the Nations Championship this Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 17/07/2026

FIJI Water Flying Fijians Acting Head Coach Senirusi Seruvakula says he is confident the players will deliver a strong performance when they take on Scotland in the Nations Championship this Sunday.

Speaking from Edinburgh, Seruvakula says the key for the team is to stay focused and make a strong start.

He says last week's result was disappointing for both the Flying Fijians and their supporters back home, but it has given the team a benchmark to assess where they are.

Insert: Seruvakula on believe – 17th July 2026

He adds that people need to be patient and believe the team is heading in the right direction.

Seruvakula also says the Flying Fijians will need to improve both their defence and attack against a Scotland side that recently faced the world's number one-ranked team.

The Flying Fijians will take on Scotland this Sunday at 1.10am.

In other matches, the All Blacks will host Ireland at 7.10pm tomorrow, Japan will take on France at 8.40pm, and the Wallabies will face Italy at 10.10pm.

On Sunday, South Africa will meet Wales at 3.40am, while England will take on Argentina at 7.10am.

item.title: Southern Zone powerhouses chase Vodafone Deans and Weet Bix Raluve national qualification
item.description: The stage is set for tomorrow’s Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union and Weet Bix Raluve semi-finals, with powerhouse schools Suva Grammar School, Marist Brothers High School, and Nasinu Secondary School all aiming to book places in the finals and secure qualification for the national championships. Grammar, Marist, and Nasinu have enjoyed strong campaigns, with the majority
item.content:encoded:
The stage is set for tomorrow’s Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Secondary Schools Rugby Union and Weet Bix Raluve semi-finals, with powerhouse schools Suva Grammar School, Marist Brothers High School, and Nasinu Secondary School all aiming to book places in the finals and secure qualification for the national championships.

Grammar, Marist, and Nasinu have enjoyed strong campaigns, with the majority of their teams advancing to the Southern Zone semi-finals.

They will now be looking to maintain that momentum and stamp their authority ahead of next week’s finals.

Each of the three schools has the maximum of four teams competing in the semi-finals, highlighting their depth and consistency across the competition.

In the Under 14 grade, Marist plays Church College Fiji at 8.30am , while Nasinu faces Dudley High School at 9.30am, both games will be held at the Veiuto Ground.

In the U15 grade, Marist faces Gospel High School at 8.30am at the Grammar Ground one, while Grammar plays Nasinu at 9.45am at the same ground.

Grammar faces MGM in the U16 grade at Grammar Ground 2 at 8.30am, while Marist plays Nasinu at 9.50am at the same ground.

In the Weet Bix Raluve competition for the U16 grade, Lomary Secondary School takes on Grammar at 10.45am at the Veiuto Ground while Nasinu faces Dudley at 11.45am at the same ground.

In the Weet Bix Raluve U18 grade, Lomary plays Ratu Latianara College at 12.45pm at Grammar ground one, while MGM plays NSS at 1.25pm at the same ground.

In the U:17 boys grade, Grammar will face Naitasiri at gr ound one at 11 am while Marist plays Gospel in ground 2.

In the feature matches in the U18 grade, MBHS will play Nasinu at 2.25pm, while Grammar will face Ratu Sukuna Memorial School at 3.50pm on the same ground.

item.title: Kadavu defeat Skipper Cup champs Malolo in another warmup match
item.description: Kadavu Rugby continued its impressive build-up to next month's Skipper Cup with another stunning upset, defeating reigning champions, Malolo 17-14 in a thrilling warm-up match today. The match attracted thousands of viewers as excitement continues to grow around the competition's newest team.
item.content:encoded:
Kadavu Rugby continued its impressive build-up to next month's Skipper Cup with another stunning upset, defeating reigning champions, Malolo 17-14 in a thrilling warm-up match today.

The match attracted thousands of viewers as excitement continues to grow around the competition's newest team.

After defeating Suva in their opening warm-up fixture last week, Kadavu backed up that performance with another statement victory, edging Malolo at Lawaqa Park.

The union has also been making history off the field.

Kadavu Rugby is the only rugby union in Fiji led by a woman president, and current Minister for Environment Lynda Tabuya, and under her leadership the team has now recorded victories over two of the country's rugby powerhouses in successive warm-up matches.

The contest lived up to expectations, with both sides displaying skill, physicality and determination in an entertaining encounter.

Kadavu's latest performance has further strengthened belief that they could emerge as one of the surprise packages when the 2026 Skipper Cup season gets underway next month.

Kadavu will face Nadi in round one of the competition in Nadi on the 1st of August.

item.title: Ba and Navua renew rivalry in crucial Extra Premier League clash
item.description: The rivalry between Ba and Navua will continue when the two sides meet in a highly anticipated Round 14 Extra Premier League clash on Sunday. The match will be a repeat of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT Final , with Ba hosting Navua at 4R Stadium in Govind Park.
item.content:encoded:
The rivalry between Ba and Navua will continue when the two sides meet in a highly anticipated Round 14 Extra Premier League clash on Sunday.

The match will be a repeat of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT Final , with Ba hosting Navua at 4R Stadium in Govind Park.

The Men in Black are currently chasing league leaders Labasa and will be hoping to collect maximum points at home.

Navua, meanwhile, has continued to improve during the season and will be looking defeat Ba.

Meanwhile, in other matches, Labasa will host Suva at 1.15pm tomorrow at Subrail Park.

On Sunday, Tailevu/Naitasiri will face Nadi at 12pm at HFC Bank Stadium, before Rewa take on Nadroga at 2pm at the same venue.

Lautoka will host Nasinu at 1pm at Churchill Park.

In the current standings, Labasa lead with 28 points , followed by Ba on 27, Rewa are third with 25 points , while Lautoka are fourth with 20 .

item.title: Pelé's 1958 World Cup Final jersey sells for US$4.88 million
item.description: The jersey worn by Brazilian football legend Pelé during the 1958 FIFA World Cup Final has sold for US$4.88 million at an auction held by Sotheby's , one of the world's leading auction houses. The shirt was worn by a 17-year-old Pelé when Brazil defeated Sweden 5-2 to win their first FIFA World Cup title.
item.content:encoded:
The jersey worn by Brazilian football legend Pelé during the 1958 FIFA World Cup Final has sold for US$4.88 million at an auction held by Sotheby's , one of the world's leading auction houses.

The shirt was worn by a 17-year-old Pelé when Brazil defeated Sweden 5-2 to win their first FIFA World Cup title.

Pelé scored twice in the final and remains the youngest player to score in a FIFA World Cup Final.

The sale is the highest price ever paid for a Pelé collectible.

Worn during Brazil's first of a record five FIFA World Cup titles, it is now the second most expensive football jersey of all time, behind Argentine legend Diego Maradona's "Hand of God" jersey, which sold for US$9.28 million after being worn during the 1986 FIFA World Cup .

Meanwhile, England will face France in the FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place play-off at 9am on Sunday.

The FIFA World Cup 2026 Final between Argentina and Spain will kick off at 7am on Monday.

item.description: Seventeen-year-old Adi Ceva Lutumailagi says she is excited to represent Fiji at her first Commonwealth Games as she prepares to c

item.description: Some England players were reportedly disappointed with manager Thomas Tuchel's tactics following their 2-1 FIFA World Cup semi-fin

item.title: MGM High School claims U15 title after edging DAV College 6-5 on penalties
item.description: MGM High School have been crowned the Suva Secondary Schools Soccer Association Under-15 champions after defeating DAV College 6-5
